content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Updated daemon lease cleanup in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs` so orphaned and terminal lease cleanup now treats `run.pid` removal as part of the durable cleanup contract: daemon-owned pid files are removed before lease metadata is cleared, dead daemon-owned pid records from prior processes are reclaimed, and any unreadable, unremovable, or still-live conflicting pid record now fails closed as `LeaseCleanupPartialFailure` instead of being silently ignored. Added focused daemon-loop regressions covering successful orphaned repair of a dead prior-daemon pid record and repo quarantine when orphaned pid cleanup cannot be safely reclaimed.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspected the Phase 0 orphaned-lease repair path and shared daemon pid-file cleanup helper to confirm that stale daemon-owned pid records from prior processes were left behind and pid read/remove failures were being suppressed.
2. [Completed] Patched `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs` so daemon lease cleanup validates pid ownership/reclaimability, removes daemon-owned pid files before clearing durable lease metadata, reclaims dead daemon pid records from prior processes, and propagates unresolved pid cleanup as `LeaseCleanupPartialFailure`.
3. [Completed] Added regression tests for dead-daemon pid reclamation and unreclaimable orphaned pid quarantine, then ran targeted and repository-wide validation commands.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo test --features test-stub process_cycle_multi_repo_reclaims_dead_daemon_pid_record_during_orphaned_repair` passed.
- `cargo test --features test-stub process_cycle_multi_repo_quarantines_unreclaimable_orphaned_pid_records` passed.
- `cargo test` passed.
- `cargo clippy` passed with only pre-existing unrelated warnings.
- `cargo fmt --check` passed.

## Outstanding Risks

- `cargo clippy` still emits pre-existing unrelated warnings (for example `proc_start_marker_raw` dead code and several existing style lints); this amendment did not introduce new clippy failures.
